content: Do a 5-phase writing task and report progress each turn using the task-progress marker. Plan exactly 5 phases (total_rounds:5). On THIS turn emit the marker at 0/5, then start phase 1: write a one-paragraph outline. End your turn after phase 1 with the marker updated to 1/5.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":0} -->
### Phase 1: One-Paragraph Outline
The task involves writing a structured document or codebase update in five phases. Phase 1 focuses on creating a concise outline to guide the subsequent phases. This outline will summarize the key points, structure, and goals of the document or code changes, ensuring clarity and alignment with the user's requirements. The outline will serve as a roadmap for the remaining phases, which will include drafting, reviewing, refining, and finalizing the content or code.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":1} -->
Continue: do phase 2 (draft the introduction), update the marker to 2/5, and stop. Do not ask a question or offer to continue — end your turn immediately after the marker.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":1} -->
### Phase 2: Draft the Introduction
The introduction will set the context for the document or codebase update, clearly stating the purpose, scope, and intended audience. It will briefly outline the problem or opportunity being addressed and provide a high-level overview of the solution or changes proposed. This section will also establish any key assumptions or constraints that will guide the subsequent phases of the task.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":2} -->
Continue: do phase 3 (draft the body), update the marker to 3/5, and stop. Do not ask a question or offer to continue — end your turn immediately after the marker.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":2} -->
### Phase 3: Draft the Body
The body of the document or codebase update will expand on the introduction by providing detailed explanations, examples, or implementations. This section will include the core content, such as technical specifications, code snippets, or step-by-step instructions, depending on the nature of the task. Each subsection will be clearly labeled and logically organized to ensure readability and coherence. Assumptions and dependencies will be explicitly noted where relevant.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":3} -->
Continue: do phase 4 (draft the conclusion), update the marker to 4/5, and stop. Do not ask a question or offer to continue — end your turn immediately after the marker.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":3} -->
### Phase 4: Draft the Conclusion
The conclusion will summarize the key points discussed in the document or codebase update, reinforcing the purpose and outcomes. It will highlight any action items, next steps, or recommendations for the user or stakeholders. Additionally, this section will address any limitations, open questions, or areas for future work, ensuring transparency and setting expectations for further development or review.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":4} -->
Finish: do phase 5 (final polish), update the marker to 5/5, and reply with exactly the word: done

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":4} -->
### Phase 5: Final Polish
The final phase involves reviewing the entire document or codebase update for clarity, consistency, and correctness. This includes proofreading for grammatical or syntactical errors, ensuring adherence to style guidelines, and validating that all sections align with the outline and introduction. Any redundant or off-topic content will be removed, and formatting will be standardized. The final output will be ready for submission or deployment.

<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":5,"completed_rounds":5} -->
done
